---
title: "Introduction"
subtitle: "Prerequisites, disclaimers, and guide overview"
---

# Introduction {.unnumbered}

*Author: [Simon Field](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fieldy6961/)*

## Overview

This guide is for R users who want to run statistical modeling, forecasting, and ML workflows **on Snowflake** — from **RStudio, Posit, VS Code**, or **Workspace Notebooks** (and optionally ML Jobs and SPCS) — without maintaining a separate R server or rewriting models in Python.

It covers three open-source packages, platform concepts for newcomers to Snowflake, local/IDE setup, notebook bootstrap, DBI connectivity, and the full ML platform (Feature Store, Model Registry, experiments, monitoring, and parallel R).

**New to Snowflake?** Start with [Snowflake Platform Primer](../01_snowflake_platform/index.qmd) and the [Glossary](../appendices/F_glossary/index.qmd).

## What's Covered in This Guide

| Ch | Topic | Description |
|----|-------|-------------|
| [**00**](index.qmd) | Introduction | Prerequisites, disclaimers, roadmap |
| [**01**](../01_snowflake_platform/index.qmd) | Snowflake primer | Platform concepts for R users |
| [**02**](../02_architecture/index.qmd) | Architecture | Three packages, rpy2, auth |
| [**03–04**](../03_local_r_setup/index.qmd) | R from your IDE | Local setup, RStudio, Posit, VS Code |
| [**05–09**](../05_workspaces_overview/index.qmd) | Workspace | Notebooks, bootstrap, EAI, `%%R`, CRE |
| [**10**](../10_multilang_beyond_r/index.qmd) | Beyond R | Julia, Scala, Java (optional) |
| [**11–14**](../11_rsnowflake_sql_api/index.qmd) | RSnowflake | SQL API, DBI, dbplyr |
| [**15–23**](../15_mlops_on_snowflake/index.qmd) | snowflakeR | MLOps, Feature Store, Registry, scale-out |
| [**F**](../appendices/F_glossary/index.qmd) | Glossary | Terms and acronyms |

## Learning Objectives

After completing this chapter, you will be able to:

- Explain who this guide is for and what problems it solves
- List prerequisites for Workspace and local development
- Locate the three package repositories and companion notebooks

---

## Important disclaimers {#sec-disclaimers}

::: {.callout-important}
## snowflakeR community project

The `snowflakeR` package gives R users an idiomatic interface to the `snowflake-ml-python` SDK and Snowflake's server-side ML capabilities. It is **not yet officially supported by Snowflake** but is developed to help customers use R in Workspaces and containers and to gather feedback. APIs may change as the package evolves.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## CRE vs bootstrap

In most enterprises, **platform or IT** first onboards a **Custom Runtime Environment (CRE)** — a pre-built image registered as `cre@<org_name>` so analysts get R and `%%R` without a setup cell ([Workspace Bootstrap ch. 6](../06_workspace_bootstrap/index.qmd#sec-cre-vs-bootstrap), [CRE ch. 9](../09_custom_runtime_and_ml_jobs/index.qmd)).

**`setup_notebook()` bootstrap** is what you use **until that org image exists**, or for your own PoC: one Python cell, no Docker — typically **~60 seconds per compute restart** for a standard package set (longer if you add many R packages or options in YAML). When a CRE is attached, the toolkit detects it (`SFNB_CUSTOM_RUNTIME`) and skips redundant installs.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Pre-built package tarballs in Workspace

Configure **pre-built `.tar.gz`** tarballs for `snowflakeR` and `RSnowflake` in your notebook YAML (`languages.r.tarballs`). Installing from source inside Workspace is slow (minutes) and brittle. See [Workspace Bootstrap](../06_workspace_bootstrap/index.qmd) and [Appendix B](../appendices/B_config_reference/index.qmd).
:::

## Prerequisites {#sec-prerequisites}

### Snowflake access

- Snowflake account with [Workspace Notebooks](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-snowsight/notebooks) (or ML Container Runtime for ML Jobs)
- Role with warehouse usage and notebook execution
- For automatic EAI creation: `CREATE INTEGRATION` (or an admin-run script from the bootstrap step)

### R and Python (local development)

| Component | Version |
|-----------|---------|
| R | 4.2+ |
| Python | 3.10+ (for `snowflake-ml-python` bridge) |
| Optional | `connections.toml` or key-pair auth for local connect |

See [Appendix A: Setup](../appendices/A_setup/index.qmd) and [Local R Setup](../03_local_r_setup/index.qmd).

### Skills

- Comfortable writing R and reading SQL
- Basic familiarity with notebooks (cells, kernels) if using Workspace
- ML concepts (features, training, inference) for snowflakeR chapters

## Companion artifacts {#sec-companions}

| Artifact | Location |
|----------|----------|
| Workspace notebooks | [snowflakeR `inst/notebooks`](https://github.com/Snowflake-Labs/snowflakeR/tree/main/inst/notebooks) |
| Multilang smoke test | [sfnb `examples/r_smoke_test`](https://github.com/Snowflake-Labs/snowflake-notebook-multilang/tree/main/examples/r_smoke_test) |
| Feature Store concepts | [Feature Store Implementation Guide](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflake-featurestore-imp-guide/) |

## Next steps {#sec-next}

[Snowflake Platform Primer](../01_snowflake_platform/index.qmd) if you are new to Snowflake, then [Architecture](../02_architecture/index.qmd).
